ai.notes: The form is built using AngularJS. The questionnaire section is dynamically rendered from a JSON object embedded in the HTML. Some fields are radio buttons or checkboxes that are not standard HTML <select> elements.

fields.question: Upload Resume
Full Name
Email Address
Phone Number
SMS Consent
What is your preferred first name, if different from above?
What are your personal pronouns?
options: Prefer not to share at this time

fields.question: Are you either a US citizen OR have a US work authorization that does not require employer sponsorship?
options: Yes, I am a citizen or have US work authorization.
No, I do not have US work authorization.

fields.question: How did you find out about this job opening? (check any that apply)
options: Referred by a Compass staff member, volunteer, donor or client
Workforce development organization (JVS, Goodwill, Upwardly Global, etc.)
Other job board (besides LinkedIn, Indeed or Handshake)
I am a current or former Compass employee or volunteer
I am a current or former Compass client

fields.question: If you were referred by a current Compass employee, please provide their name below.
How would you describe your level of Spanish proficiency?
options: Professional proficiency (able to have extended conversations and email exchanges on complex topics)
Conversational (able to have conversations about straightforward topics)

fields.question: Which of these work schedules are you open to?
Which of these San Francisco neighborhoods are you open to working in?
Are you interested in roles that are fully on-site (no remote days)?
options: Yes, I am open to working on-site every day.
No, I need one remote day per week.
No, I need at least two remote days per week.

fields.question: Which salary ranges are you open to?

Some of our roles require the ability to drive around the Bay Area, using cars rented through our Zipcar membership. Do you have a valid CA driver's license and ability to be approved as a Zipcar driver?
Are you more interested in working with adults, kids, or both?
options: I'm interested in roles where most of my time will be spent planning and leading activities for children.
I'm interested in roles where I'll be primarily working with the adult members of the family.
I'm not sure / It depends on the position.

How and where have you developed the skills that you'll need to excel in this role?

If there is any additional information that you would like to share, please do so here.
